title: Gate checkout on authenticated ready state
verify: Guest checkout stores only safe /checkout return path and routes to login.
Every state except authenticated_ready blocks checkout continuation; merge_blocked offers merge retry.
Authenticated ready consumes the return path and renders only a bounded FT-006 checkout handoff, not payment behavior.
Gate documentation/test explicitly preserves later backend customer authorization requirement.
evidence_required: Safe return path and merge-blocked retry assertions.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Only authenticated_ready may render checkout continuation.
Create no checkout fields, order, inventory, or payment behavior.
Frontend gate never replaces backend actor authorization.
invariants: Guest cannot proceed to checkout/payment continuation.
Unresolved cart merge blocks continuation without logging customer out.
Unsafe return path never controls navigation.
purpose: Enforce the buyer-facing login-before-payment boundary without implementing later checkout/payment scope.
success_outcome: Guests and unresolved auth/merge states cannot reach checkout continuation, while authenticated-ready customers receive a clean FT-006 handoff.
anti_goals: Do not implement checkout form, order, inventory, or payment.
Do not treat client state as backend authorization.
Do not broaden return redirects beyond internal paths.

stop_conditions: Checkout handoff cannot be separated from FT-006 fields or payment implementation.
Auth lifecycle lacks a truthful authenticated_ready/merge_blocked distinction.
Gate would rely on unvalidated browser flags instead of current customer state.
